description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared brass horn mounted on a curved metal arm, which is attached to a wooden base. The base is rectangular and robust, with decorative columns and a dark wood finish, giving it an antique appearance. On top of the base sits a circular turntable platter with a black vinyl record, and a metallic tonearm with a visible stylus positioned over the record. A hand-crank mechanism with a wooden handle is attached to the side, indicating manual operation. The horn is a prominent feature, with a warm, coppery patina and visible aging, adding to the object's historical character. No dust cover or modern control knobs are present, emphasizing its classic, mechanical design.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, wooden base, hand-crank, and visible tonearm over a black vinyl record. The design is ornate and antique, with a prominent flared horn and decorative woodwork.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved metal arm supporting the large brass horn, the wooden base, and the turntable with a record.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide opening of the brass horn, the turntable platter, and the hand-crank mechanism on the right.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the horn's curve, the tonearm over the record, and the hand-crank handle more prominently.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the horn, the metal support structure, and the wooden base with less detail of the turntable.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black, with a vinyl record placed on top. It is medium-sized relative to the base, with a smooth, slightly glossy finish and visible grooves on the record. The platter sits centrally on the top surface and appears to be made of metal or hard plastic beneath the record.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and slender, extending from the side of the base over the record. It is medium in length, with a joint near the base for pivoting, and is positioned so that the stylus is over the record. The finish is metallic silver, and the arm appears to be made of steel.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible, attached to the end of the tonearm, and appears as a small, pointed component. It is metallic, likely steel or another hard material, and is positioned to contact the grooves of the record.
